howItWorks.intro: This youtube revenue calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Gross Revenue = (Monthly Views x Ad Rate) x CPM / 1,000
Creator Revenue = Gross Revenue x Revenue Share %
Projected Revenue = Sum of monthly revenues with growth
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is the difference between CPM and RPM?
faq.answer: CPM (Cost Per Mille) is what advertisers pay per 1,000 ad impressions. RPM (Revenue Per Mille) is what you actually earn per 1,000 video views after YouTube takes its share and accounting for videos where no ad was shown.

faq.question: What is a typical YouTube CPM for music channels?
faq.answer: Music channels typically see CPMs of $2-$6, though it varies widely by audience geography. US and UK viewers generate higher CPMs ($5-$12) compared to developing countries ($0.50-$2).

faq.question: How does YouTube's revenue share work?
faq.answer: For standard long-form videos, creators receive 55% of ad revenue. Music content monetized through Content ID typically receives 45%. YouTube Shorts revenue is distributed from a creator pool at a different rate.
